meaning: n.大头钉,粗缝,行动方针,食物vt.以大头针钉住,附加vi.抢风航行,作文字形移动
examples: I usually tack on DS after the database name.
Products are increasingly being morphed into tack-on feature sets of bigger products.
In a market where there appears to be a surplus, the common mistake is to tack on too many unnecessary requirements.
Godfrey, 32, and cofounder Robert McLaughlin, 31, are taking a different tack from their competitors.
Hewlett-Packard ( HPQ - news - people ) took a different tack.
